wikidata_org_candidates.label: Western Electricity Coordinating Council

These are entirely different organizations with different names, industries, and timelines.

results.possibleIdentity: Roland Plottel is an American electronic engineer and intellectual property lawyer based in New York City. Born on October 1, 1934, in New York City to Charles and Frances Plottel, he received an extensive education from Columbia University, earning a Bachelor of Arts in 1955, a Bachelor of Laws in 1958, and a Master of Science in Electrical Engineering in 1964. Plottel established a career at the intersection of technology and law, notably serving as a patent attorney and engineer for Western Electric (the manufacturing arm of AT&T) at their headquarters at 195 Broadway in New York City. His technical expertise extended into the aerospace field, where he served as 'of counsel' in patent interference cases such as Polye v. Uhl (1964), which involved technologies for gyro compasses used in navigation. Plottel is also associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) movement, where he utilized his legal and technical background to assist artists with patent and invention administration, as noted in records from the New York Intellectual Property Law Association (NYIPLA). He has been an active member of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) and the American Bar Association. Alongside his wife, Professor Jeanine Parisier Plottel, he is a known supporter of the arts and philanthropic organizations such as the New York Public Library and ORT America.
